data.title: Datadog surges after Q2 beat, raises 2025 outlook
data.description:
Shares of Datadog (NASDAQ:DDOG) rose about 11% premarket on Thursday after second quarter results beat estimates and the company raised its full year outlook.

For the second quarter ended June 30, non-GAAP EPS grew about 7% year-over-year to $0.46, while revenue jumped around 28% year-over-year to $827M. Both top and bottom-line numbers beat estimates.

"At our DASH 2025 user conference, we showcased our rapid pace of innovation, announcing over 125 new innovations to help our customers observe, secure, and act on their complex cloud environments and AI tech stacks," said Datadog's Co-Founder and CEO Olivier Pomel.

As of June 30, 2025, the company had about 3,850 customers with annual recurring revenue, or ARR, of $100,000 or more, an increase of 14% from about 3,390 as of June 30, 2024.

Outlook:

Outlook Q3: Datadog, which provides a security platform for cloud applications, expects third quarter revenue to be between $847M and $851M (midpoint at $849M), which was above consensus estimate of $819.53M. The company forecast non-GAAP net income per share to be in the range of $0.44 to $0.46 (midpoint at $0.45) versus $0.40 consensus.

Outlook full year 2025: The company raised its revenue outlook and now expects between $3.312B and $3.322B (midpoint at $3.317B) versus consensus of $3.24B. During first quarter results in May, Datadog expected revenue to be between $3.215B and $3.235B (midpoint at $3.225B).

Datadog expects non-GAAP net income per share between $1.80 and $1.83 (midpoint at $1.815) versus consensus of $1.70. The company's prior forecast was a range of $1.67 and $1.71 (midpoint at $1.69).

data.title: Ligand Pharmaceuticals beats Q2 expectations, raises guidance
data.description:
• Ligand Pharmaceuticals press release (NASDAQ:LGND): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.60 beats by $0.18.
  • Revenue of $47.6M (+14.7% Y/Y) beats by $3.73M.

  • Ligand is increasing its 2025 full year financial guidance. The Company now expects total revenue of $200 million to $225 million (previously $180 million to $200 million) and is raising its guidance on adjusted earnings per diluted share1 to $6.70 to $7.00 (previously $6.00 to $6.25).

    Royalties are now expected to be in the range of $140 million to $150 million (previously $135 million to $140 million). Sales of Captisol are unchanged from $35 million to $40 million and contract revenue is now in the range of $25 million to $35 million (previously $10 million to $20 million).

data.title: Kimbell Royalty Partners reports mixed Q2 results; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Kimbell Royalty Partners press release (NYSE:KRP): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.02 misses by $0.10.
  • Revenue of $86.55M (+13.0% Y/Y) beats by $4.9M.
  • Total second quarter 2025 consolidated Adjusted EBITDA was $63.8 million (consolidated Adjusted EBITDA is a non-GAAP financial measure.
  • Second quarter 2025 run-rate average daily production was 25,355 Boe per day (6:1), which was composed of approximately 47% from natural gas (6:1) and approximately 53% from liquids (33% from oil and 20% from NGLs).
  • Kimbell affirms its financial and operational guidance ranges for 2025 previously disclosed in its Q4 2024 earnings release.
